content: Claude Code stream-json headless adapter pattern (overstory-46ad): ClaudeRuntime.buildDirectSpawn returns argv [claude, -p, --output-format stream-json, --input-format stream-json, --verbose, --strict-mcp-config, --permission-mode bypassPermissions, optional --model], mirroring Multica claude.go:416-439. parseEvents() reuses Sapling's TextDecoder + buffered split-on-newline scaffolding but transforms Claude's nested message.content[] blocks into flat AgentEvents: assistant text → assistant_message (with optional model/usage), assistant tool_use → tool_use (callId/name/input), user tool_result → tool_result (toolUseId/content), system → status (sessionId/subtype), result → result (sessionId/result/isError/durationMs/numTurns). thinking blocks and log/control_request messages are skipped. timestamp is new Date().toISOString() at emission. Do NOT set headless=true on ClaudeRuntime — per-agent opt-in is separate work (overstory-268b).

ClaudeRuntime parseEvents onSessionId hook (overstory-2916): parseEvents accepts optional opts.onSessionId callback that fires exactly once, synchronously, on the first event whose sessionId is a non-empty string. Implementation uses a closure-scoped sessionIdPinned flag and a maybePinSession helper called between emitLine output and yield. Consumer errors are wrapped in try/catch and swallowed so a thrown callback cannot crash the parser. Hook lives in the consumer loop (not emitLine) so it does not collide with sibling text-batching edits to the per-event mapping logic. Wire to SessionStore.updateClaudeSessionId for resume-on-crash session_id pinning.

ClaudeRuntime.parseEvents text-batching pattern (overstory-d854): adjacent assistant_text deltas coalesce into one assistant_message via a 500ms / 4 KiB window. Implemented as race between in-flight reader.read() and a setTimeout — wrap each in a tagged Promise<Race> ({kind:'read'}|{kind:'timeout'}) and Promise.race them. CRITICAL: on timeout, do NOT refresh readPromise; the underlying reader.read() is still in flight and must be consumed on the next loop iteration. Flush triggers: timer (since pendingStartTs), size cap (UTF-8 byte length via TextEncoder, NOT JS string length), non-text event arrival, stream end. Non-text events flush pending text first, then yield the structured event. Batch event uses start-of-window timestamp; model/usage take latest contributing message (latest wins) and are omitted when no contributing message provided them. Local-variable batch state inside the async generator (no class fields). Refactor emitLine into a sync generator processLine that yields events in order so size-cap flushes can be interleaved with the new fragment.

nudge routing pattern for headless agents: HeadlessClaudeConnection.nudge() writes a stream-json user-message envelope (type:user, message:{role,content:[{type:text,text}]}) to the process stdin. nudge.ts uses hasNudge() type guard to check getConnection() first; tmux agents have no registered connection so fall through to send-keys. NudgeableConnection extends RuntimeConnection without modifying the interface itself. Return type includes queued?: boolean to surface the stdin-buffer caveat — Claude Code does not poll stdin while an API stream is in flight.

Runtime-agnostic ov nudge: nudge.ts checks getConnection(agentName) from src/runtimes/connections.ts first; when a NudgeableConnection (hasNudge() type guard) is present, it routes through conn.nudge(text) (HeadlessClaudeConnection writes a stream-json user-message envelope to stdin and returns Queued). Otherwise it falls back to the existing resolveTargetSession + isSessionAlive + sendKeys tmux path. Debounce (500ms) and EventStore custom-event recording apply to both paths. The headless path returns queued: true on the nudgeAgent return shape because Claude Code does not reliably poll stdin while an API stream is in flight (mid-task steering caveat — see docs/direction-ui-and-ipc.md Stretch goals). Sapling-style: connection-specific protocols are the connection's responsibility, not the caller's.

nudge-rebaser cherry-pick pattern (overstory-1f66): when a builder's branch predates subsequent merges to main, the rebase agent uses git cherry-pick <commit-sha> to land the single implementation commit cleanly. Conflicts are unlikely if files were newly created in both branches and the cherry-picked commit only extends them.

hooks-headless-rebaser cherry-pick pattern (overstory-1c32): when a builder branch (hooks-headless-builder) predates subsequent merges on main, cherry-pick the single implementation commit onto a new rebaser branch. The cherry-pick is clean when the builder's changes are non-overlapping with intervening merges.

Headless Claude mail injection wiring (overstory-2a6d): src/runtimes/connections.ts exposes addHeadlessConnectionListener({ onRegister(agentName, stdin), onRemove?(agentName) }) which fires only for connections created via registerHeadlessConnection() — Sapling/setConnection() callers are NOT surfaced because their wire format differs from headless Claude's stream-json stdin. runServe() in src/commands/serve.ts uses installMailInjectors(mailDbPath) which subscribes a listener that starts startMailInjectionLoop per agent and tracks cleanup fns; the returned stop fn unsubscribes and clears all loops. Late subscribers receive an immediate onRegister catch-up for any already-registered headless agents.

name: spawn-per-turn-task-scoped-claude
description: Task-scoped headless Claude workers (builder/scout/reviewer/merger/lead) operate under spawn-per-turn (overstory-2cf9, Phase 3): no persistent process between turns. Each user turn (initial dispatch from sling, mail batch from ov serve, nudge from ov nudge) spawns a fresh claude through src/agents/turn-runner.ts with --resume <session-id>, writes the turn to a real stdin pipe, drains stream-json into events.db, captures the new session id via the parser's onSessionId hook, and exits on EOF. The runner observes the agent's capability-specific terminal mail and transitions state to completed before exit. ov sling itself drives the first turn synchronously and exits — there is no long-lived FIFO claude. Persistent capabilities (coordinator/orchestrator/monitor) keep their long-lived process via src/worktree/process.ts. Eliminates the FIFO-zombie failure pattern (mx-7339b4).

content: Capability-specific terminal mail (overstory-2cf9): the turn-runner's checkTerminalMailSince observes a different mail type per capability before transitioning state to completed. builder/scout/reviewer/lead → 'worker_done'; merger → 'merged' or 'merge_failed'. Lookup via terminalMailTypesFor(capability) in src/agents/capabilities.ts. Lead's terminal worker_done is sent AFTER all merge_ready signals; the harness gate on tracker close still enforces merge_ready ≥ worker_done received. When adding a new task-scoped capability, add its row to terminalMailTypesFor and update the agent definition's completion-protocol to send the matching mail type as its final action.

TASK_SCOPED_CAPABILITIES central registry (overstory-2cf9): src/agents/capabilities.ts exports the canonical Set of worker capabilities driven by spawn-per-turn ({builder, scout, reviewer, merger, lead}). Single source of truth — turn-runner-dispatch.isSpawnPerTurnAgent and serve.installMailInjectors's session scan both consume isTaskScopedCapability(). Phase 4 (overstory-2724) will hoist PERSISTENT_CAPABILITIES (coordinator/orchestrator/monitor) into the same module to fix the divergent definitions across log.ts, watchdog/health.ts, watchdog/daemon.ts. When changing the task-scoped vs persistent boundary, edit ONLY this module — not local copies.

turn.pid file for cross-process abort (overstory-2cf9): under spawn-per-turn, AgentSession.pid is null between turns — there is no persistent claude PID for ov stop or watchdog to signal. The turn-runner publishes the live PID to .overstory/agents/<name>/turn.pid via Bun.write on spawn and unlinks it in the outer finally (covers thrown errors as well as the happy path). ov stop reads this file (src/utils/pid.readPidFile) and prefers it over session.pid for headless agents — when found and alive, SIGTERM via killProcessTree. Sapling RPC agents still use conn.abort() via getConnection() before the PID-file path is consulted. When adding a new spawn-per-turn-driven entry point, also clean up turn.pid on agent termination.

PERSISTENT_CAPABILITIES + STOP_HOOK_PERSISTENT_CAPABILITIES are defined in src/agents/capabilities.ts as the single source of truth. PERSISTENT_CAPABILITIES = {coordinator, orchestrator, monitor} (used by watchdog/health.ts and watchdog/daemon.ts for stale/zombie exemption and run-completion accounting). STOP_HOOK_PERSISTENT_CAPABILITIES = PERSISTENT_CAPABILITIES + lead (used by commands/log.ts to gate transitionToCompleted, autoRecordExpertise, and appendOutcomeToAppliedRecords against per-turn Stop hook fires). The two-set design preserves overstory-49a7 (tmux-mode leads span many model turns within a single dispatch, so per-turn Stop hooks are NOT a 'done' signal) while still excluding leads from run-completion accounting (leads exit after their terminal worker_done). No more local PERSISTENT_CAPABILITIES copies — Phase 4 / overstory-2724.

SessionStore.tryTransitionState (overstory-a993): use the matrix-guarded CAS for any writer that races with another writer to session.state — namely the turn-runner end-of-turn settle (src/agents/turn-runner.ts), ov stop (src/commands/stop.ts), the watchdog terminate paths (src/watchdog/daemon.ts), and the PreToolUse hook revival in src/commands/log.ts. The matrix lives in src/sessions/store.ts (TRANSITION_ALLOWED_FROM): completed is sticky (no transition out), zombie is sticky except ov stop may promote to completed, and booting → working can never undo a watchdog zombie call. The unguarded updateState() is reserved for forced cleanup paths (clean.ts, sling.ts spawn-failure, supervisor/coordinator/monitor self-management). Implementation is a per-target prepared UPDATE with WHERE state IN (...allowed-from-literals...); db.changes === 0 means rejected. tryTransitionState returns { ok, prev, next } | { ok: false, reason: 'not_found' | 'illegal_transition', prev, attempted } so callers can resync local mirrors and log diagnostics.

name: tmux-wrapper-exec-prefix
description: Tmux session wrappers prepend 'exec' before the spawned command so bash replaces itself with the runtime process. Why: without exec, a separate bash wrapper PID survives and orphans the runtime child if the tmux server is killed externally; with exec, SIGHUP is delivered directly. How to apply: use the pattern in src/worktree/tmux.ts createSession when wrapping any command with bash export/unset setup. (overstory-505d)

content: isHeadless flag in HooksDef skips settings.local.json deployment for headless Claude agents. deployConfig in claude.ts guards deployHooks() with !hooks.isHeadless. sling.ts hoists resolveUseHeadless before deployConfig so both share the same value.
